revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23984597483|manual triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:神門古墳群]]
{{Infobox ancient site |name=Gōdo Kofun Cluster |alternate_name= |image=[[File:Godo Kofun Group-5, funkyu.jpg|250px]] |caption=Gōdo No. 5 Kofun |location={{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|qid=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture|1=Chiba Prefecture}}, {{ill|Ichihara, Chiba|en|Ichihara, Chiba|qid=Q207881|lt=Ichihara|1=Ichihara, Chiba}}, Sōja; {{ill|Sōja|qid=Q28689401|1=Sōja}}|coordinates={{Coord|35|29|39.5|N|140|6|36.7|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}}; {{Coord|35.49152778|N|140.11019444|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|Time built=Mid to late {{ill|3rd century|en|3rd century|qid=Q8101|lt=3rd century|1=3rd century}} |burial goods=Swords, spears, beads, iron arrowheads |designation1=No. 5 Kofun: Chiba Prefectural Historic Site |map_size=Three {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|纒向型前方後円墳|1=Makimuku-type keyhole-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōen-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11608504}}, three {{ill|Hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōfun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, one {{ill|Entōfun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Entōfun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |map={{location map|Japan Chiba|map_dot_label=Gōdo Kofun Cluster|coordinates=35|coordinates=29|coordinates=39.5|lon_deg=140|lon_min=06|lon_sec=36.7|marksize=10|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{Coord|35|29|39.5|N|140|6|36.7|E}}}}}}
'''Gōdo Kofun Cluster''' (神門古墳群) is a group of ancient Kofuns located in {{ill|Ichihara, Chiba|en|Ichihara, Chiba|qid=Q207881|lt=Ichihara|1=Ichihara, Chiba}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|qid=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture|1=Chiba Prefecture}}, in the Sōja area. Among these, the No. 5 Kofun has been Historic Site1 as a {{ill|Prefectural Historic Site|ja|県指定史跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Historic Site|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} by Chiba Prefecture.<ref name=Chiba>{{Cite web|author=Chiba Prefectural Board of Education designation1 Division|url=https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/p411-072.html|title=Gōdo No. 5 Kofun|publisher=Chiba Prefecture|accessdate=2020-05-24}}</ref>
== Overview == [[File:神門3号墳 出土品 (市原歴史博物館展示).JPG|thumb|240x240px|burial goods from No. 3 Kofun, displayed at {{ill|Ichihara History Museum|en|Ichihara History Museum|qid=Q115253922|lt=Ichihara History Museum|1=Ichihara History Museum}}]] The cluster is located on the northern banks of the {{ill|Yōrō River|en|Yōrō River|qid=Q8062667|lt=Yōrō River|1=Yōrō River}}, at the western edge of the Ichihara Plateau. It consists of three {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} (keyhole-shaped tumuli), three {{ill|Hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōfun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (square mounds), and one {{ill|Entōfun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Entōfun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (round mound).
=== Key Tumuli === ==== No. 3 Kofun ==== Excavated in 1987. The mound measures 47.5 meters in restored length (53.5 meters as reconstructed), with a circular rear section of 33.5 meters in diameter, a front projection of 20 meters long and 15 meters wide, and a height of 5.2 meters. Recovered burial goods include one iron sword, one iron spear, two iron arrowheads, one woodworking adze, ten tubular beads, and 103 small glass beads.
==== No. 4 Kofun ==== Excavated in 1975. The mound measures 49 meters in length, with a circular rear section of 33 meters long and 30 meters wide, a front projection of 13 meters long and wide, and a height of 4.3 meters. burial goods include one iron sword, 41 iron arrowheads, 31 tubular beads, 394 small glass beads, one iron spear, and one woodworking adze.
==== No. 5 Kofun ==== Excavated in 1948 and 1983. The tumulus measures 36.5 meters in restored length (42.5 meters as reconstructed), with a circular rear section of 31.5 meters long and 32 meters wide, a front projection of 11 meters long and 12 meters wide, and a height of 5.9 meters. burial goods include one iron sword, two iron arrowheads, and six glass beads.
These mounds represent early-stage {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|纒向型前方後円墳|1=Makimuku-type keyhole-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōen-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11608504}}, with No. 5 being the oldest, followed by Nos. 4 and 3. They are dated to the mid-to-late {{ill|3rd century|en|3rd century|qid=Q8101|lt=3rd century|1=3rd century}}, though some theories suggest they may date back to the early 3rd century, associating them with the {{ill|Yamatai|en|Yamatai|qid=Q844295|lt=Yamatai|1=Yamatai}} period.
<gallery> File:神門3号墳 Excavated Artifacts.JPG|burial goods from No. 3 Kofun </gallery>
== Historic Site == === Chiba Prefectural Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''' ** Gōdo No. 5 Kofun: Designated on March 10, 1989.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * {{Cite book|author1=Hatsushige Ōtsuka|author2=Saburō Kobayashi|title=Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|publisher=[[Tokyo-dō Publishing|ja|東京堂出版]]|year=2002|pages=184-185|isbn=4490105991}}
== External Links == * [http://www.city.ichihara.chiba.jp/maibun/isekifile65.htm Gōdo Kofun Cluster - Ichihara City Historic Site Research Center] * [https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/p411-072.html Gōdo No. 5 Kofun - Chiba Prefecture]
{{Kofun navbar}}
{{translated page|ja|神門古墳群|version=102235339|comment=Imported full ja history}}
